generated_oml:
// C1: System Under Study
instance ThermalIncubatorSystem : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "Cyber-Physical System (CPS) / Physical Twin (PT) for thermal regulation and environmental control, maintaining specific temperature inside an insulated container regardless of internal content."
]

instance InsulatedContainer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "A styrofoam box providing thermal insulation to maintain internal temperature."
    base:isContainedIn ThermalIncubatorSystem
]

instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Heating element to raise the temperature inside the insulated container. Controlled by Raspberry Pi with power constraints based on thermal capacity."
    base:isContainedIn ThermalIncubatorSystem
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical actuator distributing heated air evenly inside the box to mitigate thermal gradients. Operates within mechanical speed limits and acoustic noise thresholds."
    base:isContainedIn ThermalIncubatorSystem
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision DS18S20 digital thermometer measuring internal temperature of the incubator with ±0.5°C accuracy (-55°C to +125°C range)."
    base:isContainedIn ThermalIncubatorSystem
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision DS18S20 digital thermometer measuring internal temperature of the incubator with ±0.5°C accuracy (-55°C to +125°C range)."
    base:isContainedIn ThermalIncubatorSystem
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision DS18S20 digital thermometer monitoring ambient temperature outside the container with ±0.5°C accuracy (-55°C to +125°C range)."
    base:isContainedIn ThermalIncubatorSystem
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Embedded controller executing closed-loop control algorithms (PID/model-based) for temperature regulation. Interfaces with RabbitMQ for remote commands and status updates."
    base:isContainedIn ThermalIncubatorSystem
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
    base:desc "Raw temperature readings from internal sensor 1 transmitted via RabbitMQ with analog/digital conversion handled by low-level driver."
]

instance InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
    base:desc "Raw temperature readings from internal sensor 2 transmitted via RabbitMQ with analog/digital conversion handled by low-level driver."
]

instance ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Raw ambient temperature readings transmitted via RabbitMQ with analog/digital conversion handled by low-level driver."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance TwoParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simplified behavioral model (likely first-order differential equation) capturing core thermal behavior but lacking granularity in heatbed thermal inertia."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance FourParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Accurate behavioral model (potentially second-order differential equation) improving prediction fidelity by accounting for heatbed thermal inertia."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance InternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature measurements from internal sensors used for model calibration and control decisions."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted1, InternalTemperatureDataTransmitted2
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, StateEstimationEnabler
]

instance ExternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time ambient temperature measurements providing environmental context for model corrections."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ExternalTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance CalibratedModelParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Updated model parameters derived from calibration algorithms using real-time sensor data to align simulated behavior with Physical Twin."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateEstimationEnabler, ControlOptimizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQMessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source message broker facilitating asynchronous communication between low-level driver and Digital Twin components for real-time data transmission."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, ControlAndDecisionSupportService
]

instance ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Calibration algorithms updating models with real-time sensor data to improve prediction accuracy (e.g., least-squares or Bayesian inference)."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationAndPredictionService, OptimizationService
]

instance StateEstimationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Algorithms estimating system states (e.g., Kalman filters) for sensor fusion and real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance ControlOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization algorithms adjusting control inputs (e.g., heatbed power, fan speed) to maintain target temperature and energy efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlAndDecisionSupportService, OptimizationService
]

instance ModelAtRuntimeEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dynamic adaptation of models during system operation to maintain accuracy and robustness against environmental changes."
    DTDFVocab:enables ReconfigurabilityAndRobustnessService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous tracking of system performance and environmental conditions using real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeTemperatureInsight, SensorAnomalyInsight
]

instance SimulationAndPredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Running simulations to reproduce and predict the Physical Twin's behavior under current or hypothetical conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalBehaviorPredictionInsight, ModelAccuracyInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Optimizing system performance (e.g., energy efficiency, temperature stability) and generating recommendations for adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyInsight, ControllerTuningRecommendation
]

instance ControlAndDecisionSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Supervisory control detecting violations (e.g., unsafe temperatures) and triggering automated interventions."
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyViolationInsight, ActuationCommandAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Advanced visualizations of system states, simulations, and predictions (e.g., temperature gradients, control signals)."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureGradientVisualizationInsight, SystemStateDashboardInsight
]

instance ReconfigurabilityAndRobustnessService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Adapting to changing environments (e.g., external temperature fluctuations) and improving robustness through dynamic model updates."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnvironmentalAdaptationInsight, ModelUpdateRecommendation
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeTemperatureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current thermal state of the incubator (internal/external temperatures) for immediate operational awareness."
]

instance SensorAnomalyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of sensor anomalies or control failures affecting data integrity."
]

instance ThermalBehaviorPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted thermal dynamics under varying conditions (e.g., content changes inside the box)."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comparison of two-parameter vs. four-parameter model accuracy for thermal inertia prediction."
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized energy consumption recommendations based on current thermal load."
]

instance ControllerTuningRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated recommendations for adjusting controller parameters (e.g., PID gains)."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SafetyViolationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of unsafe or suboptimal performance (e.g., temperature thresholds breached)."
]

instance ActuationCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated commands to adjust heatbed power or fan speed based on control logic."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance TemperatureGradientVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of temperature distribution inside the incubator."
]

instance SystemStateDashboardInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comprehensive dashboard showing PT/DT states, control signals, and operational metrics."
]

instance EnvironmentalAdaptationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "System's ability to adapt to external temperature fluctuations and maintain performance."
]

instance ModelUpdateRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommendations for dynamic model updates to maintain accuracy under changing conditions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in the incubator DT system is mediated by the **controller** (a **bang-bang controller variant**) on the Raspberry Pi, which executes control logic based on temperature measurements. The controller **issues commands** to the **low-level driver** via the **RabbitMQ server** to actuate the **heatbed** and **fan** based on predefined parameters: **LL (Lower Limit)**, **UL (Upper Limit)**, **H (Heating Duration)**, and **C (Waiting Duration)**. The controller **does not generate commands directly from the DT**; instead, it processes sensor data and enforces control logic autonomously. The **low-level driver** validates and executes these commands, ensuring the PT remains in a **safe state**. No explicit **authentication mechanisms**, **feedback systems**, or **command execution confirmation** protocols are described. The interaction is **unidirectional** (controller → low-level driver → PT), with no mention of bidirectional validation or conflict resolution."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the incubator case study operates with a **real-time synchronization mechanism** between the Physical Twin (PT) and Digital Twin (DT) components. The low-level driver periodically reads temperature data from the **three sensors** (two inside the insulated container and one outside) to ensure temporal alignment with the PT's state. The synchronization is facilitated via a **RabbitMQ server**, which acts as a message broker to relay sensor data and controller commands with minimal latency. The temporal granularity is dictated by the **discrete control loop** of the **bang-bang controller**, which adjusts the heatbed based on threshold-based temperature readings. While no explicit synchronization frequency is specified, the system is designed to support **real-time visualization** of the incubator status, implying sub-second to second-level granularity for data acquisition and processing. The calibration process (e.g., for Model B) may introduce additional temporal overhead, but the primary operational time scale aligns with the controller's discrete actuation cycle and sensor polling interval."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the incubator follows a **Cyber-Physical System (CPS)-based architecture**, integrating **physical components** (insulated container, heatbed, fan, sensors) with **cyber components** (Raspberry Pi controller, RabbitMQ message broker, simulation models). The orchestration is structured around three core layers: **(1) Data Acquisition**, where sensors provide real-time temperature readings to the cyber layer via RabbitMQ; **(2) Model Execution**, where calibrated models (Model A/B) simulate the PT’s thermal behavior; and **(3) Control & Optimization**, where a **bang-bang controller** adjusts the heatbed based on model outputs and sensor feedback. The system employs **co-simulation techniques** (implied by 'what-if simulations' in future work) to validate controller parameters and optimize performance, though explicit co-simulation frameworks are not detailed. Calibration algorithms dynamically update model parameters to maintain alignment with the PT’s evolving state, while the RabbitMQ broker ensures low-latency communication between components. The architecture lacks explicit mention of distributed orchestration or microservices but relies on a **monolithic yet modular** design centered on the Raspberry Pi."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle for the incubator case study includes the following phases: **(1) System Definition and Modeling**: Development of abstract representations (models) of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment, including **two simulation models** (one with **two free parameters** and another with **four free parameters**) to predict temperature dynamics. **(2) Calibration and Validation**: Models are calibrated using **calibration algorithms** to update parameters based on real-time sensor data (e.g., temperature sensors inside/outside the insulated container) to ensure accuracy. **(3) Integration and Deployment**: Models are integrated into the DT framework alongside **monitoring algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) and **control algorithms** (e.g., supervisory controllers) to enable real-time synchronization with the Physical Twin (PT). **(4) Operational Phase**: The DT operates in parallel with the PT, using **data-driven algorithms** (e.g., optimizers) to generate insights, detect violations (e.g., safety or performance deviations), and trigger corrective actions via human or automated interventions. **(5) Evolution and Maintenance**: The DT supports **reconfigurability** to adapt to environmental changes (e.g., varying thermal loads) and **reduced maintenance** through predictive analytics. Transition management between phases involves **continuous calibration** and **model updates** to maintain fidelity with the PT’s evolving state. Governance is implicit in the DT’s closed-loop architecture, where **monitoring algorithms** enforce safety and optimality constraints."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The DT’s **fidelity and validity** are governed by:
  - **Model Trade-offs**: The **four-parameter model** demonstrates superior accuracy in predicting **thermal inertia** (e.g., heatbed response dynamics) compared to the two-parameter model, as validated through experimental comparisons with real-time data trajectories. However, increased model complexity may introduce computational overhead during simulation.
  - **Validation Methodology**: Models are validated by **direct comparison with empirical data** obtained from running experiments on the PT. The **non-linear least squares calibration** ensures parameter estimates minimize the discrepancy between model predictions and observed sensor readings.
  - **Uncertainty Sources**: Primarily stem from **sensor inaccuracies (±0.5°C)**, **model simplifications** (e.g., uniform temperature distribution assumption), and **environmental disturbances** (e.g., external temperature fluctuations). No explicit uncertainty quantification (e.g., confidence intervals, Monte Carlo methods) or error correction mechanisms (e.g., Kalman filters) are documented.
  - **Quality Assurance**: Relies on **experimental validation** and **parameter calibration** via least squares. The DT framework assumes the calibrated models remain valid under operating conditions, with no mention of runtime adaptation or cross-validation against independent datasets. Safety and robustness are indirectly ensured by the **bang-bang controller’s** threshold-based logic, which prevents extreme deviations from target temperatures."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical connection** for the incubator DT relies on a **RabbitMQ message broker**, deployed as a **lightweight, open-source message broker** for synchronization and communication. The **network architecture** is **centralized**, with the **controller** and **low-level driver** communicating via RabbitMQ. The **communication protocol** is not explicitly specified beyond RabbitMQ’s default **AMQP (Advanced Message Queuing Protocol)**. No details are provided on **bandwidth/latency requirements**, **reliability mechanisms** (e.g., redundancy), or **security protocols** (e.g., encryption, access control). The system assumes a **local deployment** (Raspberry Pi + RabbitMQ) with no mention of cloud integration or distributed topologies."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **hosting deployment** for the incubator DT is **on-premise**, with critical components running on a **Raspberry Pi**. The **controller** and **low-level driver** execute locally, while the **RabbitMQ server** serves as the communication backbone. No explicit **computational/storage requirements** (e.g., CPU, memory) are specified beyond the Raspberry Pi’s capabilities. The system does not mention **scalability mechanisms** (e.g., clustering, horizontal scaling), **deployment models** (e.g., containerization, microservices), or **availability/reliability assurances** (e.g., failover, persistence). The deployment is **monolithic** and **single-node**, with no redundancy or distributed deployment strategies described."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system for the incubator case study incorporates **safety-critical considerations** through its **monitoring and control mechanisms**, as depicted in the schematic overview (Fig. 1). The system employs **calibration algorithms** to update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment, ensuring accurate parameter estimation for safe and optimal operation. **Fault detection** is integrated via monitors that verify system performance against predefined safety thresholds, triggering corrective actions (e.g., adjustments to the physical twin or alerts to human operators) when violations are detected. The **Raspberry Pi-based controller** in the incubator’s hardware setup (Fig. 2) likely implements **fail-safe mechanisms** to mitigate risks, such as overheating or sensor failures, by enforcing predefined safety limits (e.g., maximum temperature thresholds). However, explicit details on **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., encryption, access control, authentication), **risk assessment methodologies**, or **compliance frameworks** (e.g., ISO 26262, IEC 61508) are not provided in the documents. The system’s reliance on **sensor data** (two internal temperature sensors, one external) and **model calibration** suggests a focus on **data integrity** for safety, but no explicit **privacy protection measures** (e.g., anonymization, consent management) or **data sharing agreements** are mentioned."
]
